decisions.summary: Resolve better-sqlite3 in the parent process via createRequire(import.meta.url) + require.resolve, then pass the absolute path into the worker via workerData. The worker uses require(workerData.betterSqlite3Path) and never touches process.cwd().

Apply the identical fix to both worker spawn sites: src/lib/registry/feature-registry-storage.ts and src/lib/memory/fts-db.ts. Both use the same broken createRequire(process.cwd() + '/package.json') pattern and both fail under the same condition.

Keep the worker spawn shape (new Worker(source, { eval: true })) — only add a workerData field. Do not switch to a file-based worker; the inline-eval approach is fine once the module resolution is fixed.

Add a regression test that loads the workers with process.cwd set to a directory outside the repo (e.g. os.tmpdir()) and asserts the workers still resolve better-sqlite3 and complete a basic operation. This prevents the bug from regressing if anyone re-introduces a cwd-based resolution.

hazards.summary: When packaged via tsdown/rolldown into dist/, import.meta.url for feature-registry-storage points inside the bundled output, not into the source tree. require.resolve('better-sqlite3') from a createRequire built on that URL must still locate node_modules above the bundle.
hazards.mitigation: Test the fix against the built dist (npm run build && node dist/dashboard/server.js) from a cwd outside the repo before declaring done. Both source-mode (tsx) and dist-mode must work.

hazards.summary: If require.resolve('better-sqlite3') fails in the parent process (e.g. the module is missing from node_modules), we currently surface a cryptic error inside the worker. Resolving in the parent lets us surface a clear error before spawning.
hazards.mitigation: When resolving in the parent, wrap require.resolve in try/catch and throw a descriptive Error('Cannot find better-sqlite3 in <parent-search-path>'). This makes future regressions obvious.

hazards.summary: A separate agent is reportedly cleaning up PAN-1204 (the PR that introduced the feature-registry worker). If their cleanup touches feature-registry-storage.ts, this fix could merge-conflict.
hazards.mitigation: Keep the diff to feature-registry-storage.ts surgical — only the worker spawn path. Do not refactor unrelated code in the same bead.

hazards.summary: The fts-db worker is on the memory FTS code path; if it crashes during dashboard boot it may also be silently degrading pan memory search. The bug report only mentions session-context, but fts-db may already be broken with the same MODULE_NOT_FOUND.
hazards.mitigation: Grep dashboard logs for 'memory.*MODULE_NOT_FOUND' / 'fts.*better-sqlite3' before and after the fix to confirm both workers are now healthy.

sessionHistory.note: Initial planning session — discovered identical bug in two worker spawn sites (feature-registry-storage.ts and fts-db.ts). Root cause and fix identified.
